preamble: The raising of the near-open front unrounded vowel [æ] to the open-mid front vowel [ɛ] is a common step in front vowel chain shifts. This Raising often occurs in modern English dialects, where short front vowels shift upwards in the vowel space.

examples.note: Raising of /æ/ is characteristic of the Southern Vowel Shift, often accompanied by diphthongization.
In the New Zealand short front vowel shift, the TRAP vowel [æ] raises to occupy the DRESS vowel space [ɛ].
Similar to NZ English, South African English shows Raising of the TRAP vowel.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction.
